hawkish_phrases: if economic developments were better than expected and favourable financing conditions could be maintained with asset purchase flows that did not exhaust the envelope
a noticeable slowing of the pace of purchases for the next quarter was seen as inappropriate at the current juncture
concerns were expressed about potential side effects if the highly accommodative monetary policy stance was maintained much longer
an increase in bankruptcies in the corporate sector could be expected later this year
dovish_phrases: net purchases under the PEPP over the next quarter to continue to be conducted at a significantly higher pace than during the first months of the year
the projected path for the economy was subject to significant uncertainties and risks
the June staff projections still see inflation falling short of the pre-pandemic projected path at the policy-relevant horizon
monetary policy had to look through temporary short-term developments in inflation and focus on the medium-term outlook
An undue tightening of financing conditions at the current juncture could jeopardise the ongoing economic recovery
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged and confirmed continuation of PEPP purchases at a significantly higher pace than in the first months of the year, maintaining an accommodative stance to preserve favourable financing conditions despite an improved growth outlook and inflation reaching 2.0%, with broad consensus supporting the decision.
